extraction.one_line_summary: ŠABUZAN Lorenzo won after UAE/AG2R trains neutralized GORDO's 250m-to-go attack on 12-13% finishing ramps, exploiting left-side road space compression.
tactical_patterns: Deceuninck-QuickStep overcooked domestique workload chasing break pre-10km, compromising ALMEIDA's sprint train for Cote de Stavelot finish.
GORDO attacked 250m out on 12% ramps, but AG2R chase gave UAE free leadout exposing French team's tactical naivety.
Left-hand road compression after final 250m turn created passing lane while right side was blocked by barging sprinters.

rider_signals.GORDO David: positive: Still dangerous enough to force chase on 12% gradients despite not being designated leader for Groupama-FDJ
rider_signals.HIRSCHI Marc: negative: Lost podium to bar-room battling despite positioning, suggests struggles in physical sprint finishes even on punchy terrain
rider_signals.ALMEIDA João: neutral: Won bonus seconds but team burned matches early chasing break, raising sustainability questions for week-long Luxembourg format
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: positive: Top-five on punchy 12-13% finish indicates resurgent climbing punch beyond pure mountain stages
